_doc: Dogfood expectations for the lantern corpus. One corpus, one truth: consumed by (1) recall golden tests, (2) the gate DOGFOOD step in scripts/verify.sh, (3) the Milestone 1 demo. Schema: entries[] of { query: str, expect_top: [ids acceptable at rank 1], expect_in_top_k: [{id, k}], forbid_in_top_k: [{id, k}], expect_zero_hits: bool, phase: 'milestone' | 'post-milestone', note: str }. All fields except query and phase are optional. forbid entries are negative cases; expect_zero_hits demands an empty result.

entries.query: which branch do we sync to
what do I run before committing

entries.note: resolved ONLY via code-aware subtokens of the camelCase symbol parseEventStream

entries.query: what did the last session change in the tokenizer
where does the default config live
where do built binaries end up
how do we version a release
entries.note: the release tag and the version body token carry this one today

near-synonym case reserved for the hash-embedding rerank bead: 'db' has no exact-token bridge to 'database'/'sqlite' — BM25 returns zero hits today (verified); only a semantic rerank layer can close it
